title: --env looses embedded newlines
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I want to pass an RSA key into a container via an environment variable.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ``$ openssl genrsa -out key.txt 512``

2. ``$ KEY="$(cat key.txt)"``

3. ``$ sudo podman run -it --rm -e "KEY=$KEY" fedora bash``

**Describe the results you received:**

```
[root@497de84146bf /]# echo "$KEY"
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
[root@497de84146bf /]# 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
[root@497de84146bf /]# echo "$KEY"
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIBOwIBAAJBALaz/aMvyWihTDeQ4/590ScgS8xcrh5MPKNzaPd3uSwqKtTVWgB8
/OrBzXkH5MeaiCLfapv7lsodH0KCRgYuvD8CAwEAAQJBALXfUPKchFtyjaOWyU8y
Qf036hyCehXRAGAZZ/lIpf2uQPRTpLNgV8NDRfL/FNAkPm8UhpLjOBuSmAQtC0um
emECIQDk8vl6aIlvlPXblDqH60CnsnqPVSUzcoOMP/U1qR5TbwIhAMxKNleGEcM+
vCdckEYt0FRJeL2xGeClLeB5py4w+XwxAiBv9gZcfxyerXjhzYYmC7yNhbF7wIa1
tw1vm6Ndf3uuDwIhAMi9S7SpSx79sElFTYeIUSPhpkFPm0wBQ5lGMR7UhPKhAiBP
jfwmzKNjdYaW9aV1Ps3vEUny9yNp8+mVLrWOIW9eSw==
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
[root@497de84146bf /]# 
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.1.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
Git Commit:         a95a49d3038462d033f84ac314ec8a3064a99cff
Built:              Tue Mar  5 13:10:31 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: a95a49d3038462d033f84ac314ec8a3064a99cff
  go version: go1.11.5
  podman version: 1.1.2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.7.1
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.1.2-1.git0ad9b6b.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: a95a49d3038462d033f84ac314ec8a3064a99cff'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "29"
  MemFree: 1238302720
  MemTotal: 12437794816
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-83.dev.gitf56b4cb.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: cb19e6a579f2a7da040525d725cfb871b4275148
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 831557632
  SwapTotal: 1073737728
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: ...cut...
  kernel: 4.20.16-200.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 266h 42m 40s (Approximately 11.08 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - localhost
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/cevich/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/cevich/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: ...cut...
  VolumePath: /home/cevich/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Also tried ``sudo podman run -it --rm -e "KEY='$KEY'" fedora bash`` which gives exact same (bad) output, except starts with a ``'`` character.  i.e. podman seems to be choking on the newlines.

comments.body:
I just tried with just -e KEY
sudo podman run -it --rm -e KEY
And this seems to work.
I think this could be shell removing the new lines.

Seems to be working in master

 ./bin/podman run -ti -e dan1="${dan}" alpine sh
/ # echo "$dan1"
Dan
Walsh
/ # exit

Tested with alpine, but the issue still reproduces for me.  Also tested with embedding the quote within the -e argument ``so -e KEY="$KEY"`` vs ``-e "KEY=$KEY"``.  Still seems to be a problem.  Perhaps what was fixed in #2810 also resolves this?  Will wait for new fedora builds and confirm...
Yeah, I'd try reproducing on 1.2.0 builds

Confirmed: This is fixed in 1.2.0.

@mheon Is this something worth adding a regression test for, or is the fix more "permanent"?
I think this was a one-time thing (I don't see us migrating changing CLI libraries again any time soon!) but I will never say no to more regression tests

@mheon Ahh okay, ya, probably won't ever see it again then.  Probably not worth my time to add it, will leave adding it as as an exercise for someone less involved with other items :smile:   Thx for your opinion.
